mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows and signals accommodative monetary policy, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for month-over-month FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency in easing months is not significantly above 50 percent (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.5 percent signals deep labor market distress that erodes aggregate demand and compresses corporate pricing power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Episodes with UNRATE above 6.5 percent are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and retail sales confirm a coordinated contraction in both supply and final demand, triggering broad-based earnings downgrades and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month declines in INDPRO and RSAFS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During high-unemployment regimes, a month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals the start of labor market repair that raises expectations for cyclical revenue recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE exceeds 6 percent, month-over-month declines in UNRATE are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales captures real-time consumer spending momentum that immediately impacts revenue forecasts for consumer-facing corporations, driving positive 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month changes show a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window for 5-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month cumulative decline in industrial production exceeding 2 percent signals severe manufacturing contraction and inventory liquidation, prompting institutional de-risking and negative 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO 3-month cumulative declines exceeding 2 percent are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month changes in nonfarm payrolls reflect sustained labor income growth that supports aggregate consumption and corporate earnings over quarterly horizons, generating positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window for 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms aggregate demand expansion and capacity utilization recovery, leading to broad upward earnings revisions and positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth shows a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window for 60-day forward returns.
